recommendations.whyNeeded: Line-break and relative-position capabilities need a deterministic defensive-line reference before exposing user-facing line-break concepts.
recommendations.outputType: frame_signal or anchor-local record containing line_x_m, line_type, defender_ids_used, quality flags, and confidence-free deterministic evidence.
recommendations.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN when orientation, defender identity, or sufficient outfield-player positions are unavailable under declared quality rules.
validationPlan: Positive tests for clear back-line and second-line frames.
Negative tests for non-line player distributions.
UNKNOWN tests for missing orientation or insufficient defenders.
Visual overlays for representative positive, negative, and borderline examples.

recommendations.whyNeeded: Line breaks, runs behind the last line, rest-defence framing, and support-after-line-break questions need player/ball position relative to a deterministic line model.
recommendations.outputType: frame_signal or anchor-relative record containing line_x_m, entity_x_relative_m, side_of_line, line_type, and evidence entity IDs
recommendations.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN when the line model is UNKNOWN, entity positions are missing, or attacking direction cannot be resolved.
visualEvidence: source defender IDs used for line estimate
validationPlan: Unit-test orientation-normalized ahead/behind signs.
Golden-frame tests for clear back-line and second-line cases.
UNKNOWN tests for missing orientation, line model, and entity positions.
Replay overlays for positive, negative, and borderline examples.

recommendations.whyNeeded: The second tactical family needs a reviewed, measurable line-break anchor instead of ad hoc x-thresholds or subjective labels.
recommendations.outputType: episode_set<anchor_ref> with break_frame_id, breaking_entity_id, crossed_line_type, pre/post relative positions, and control evidence
recommendations.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN when possession/control, line model, entity tracking, or before/after window completeness cannot be established.
validationPlan: Positive examples for controlled line breaks.
Negative examples for uncontrolled clearances or no crossing.
UNKNOWN tests for incomplete before/after windows.
Visual replay evidence for each accepted anchor.

recommendations.whyNeeded: General lane occupancy is needed for complementary lanes, support diversity, wide isolation, and line-break response recipes.
recommendations.outputType: episode_set or frame_signal records with lane_id, lane_bounds, occupying_entity_ids, occupied_lane_count, and distinct_lane_count
recommendations.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN when player positions, pitch dimensions, attacking direction, or team selectors are unavailable for the evaluated frame/window.
validationPlan: Boundary tests at central/half-space/wide lane edges.
Count tests with synthetic frame slices from real canonical rows.
UNKNOWN tests for missing player frame data.
Replay overlay review for at least three matches.

recommendations.whyNeeded: Several tactical questions ask whether teammates arrive after an anchor; this should be an anchor-relative relation with explicit delay, distance/lane criteria, and witness players.
recommendations.outputType: relation_episode_set with arrival_frame_id, arrival_delay_seconds, support_player_id, support_lane, support_distance_m, and anchor_evaluations
recommendations.unknownBehavior: UNKNOWN when anchor/player frames are unavailable or support criteria cannot be evaluated through the full window.
validationPlan: Positive tests for arrivals inside the window.
Negative tests for no arrival and late arrival.
UNKNOWN tests for incomplete anchor windows.
Replay examples showing distinct support lanes.

excludedNearTermCandidates.reason: Valuable after defensive-line-relative position exists, but lower priority than support, lane, local-number, and pressure capabilities for the second tactical family.
Current anchor-relative relation pattern can express the next needed temporal questions without redesigning the IR.
Unavailable from current tracking data and explicitly outside the current deterministic evidence boundary.
Deferred until wide-isolation, pressing, and counterpress queries justify the exact deterministic metric.

summary.implementationPosture: Record as the likely second tactical-family package only; do not implement during this audit and do not redesign the query IR first.

tacticalKnowledgePack.reconciliation: The external review referenced 44a9b5fb3f3748c0e8a7bc80a4134fe3cb062dd66807ca20a671cb980529b7c6, which is recorded in delivery/ledger.jsonl and docs/reviews/2026-06-21-m1-2-s2i-a-local-verification.md for an earlier S2I-A local-verification state. The clean committed source snapshot audited here is 7ec9163a6d3fcd2e1c4f197f1a2d8da24de44341, where generated/tactical-knowledge-pack.json hashes to 28405b82d54b961459842d77f1547e3a37fa5bb1ce2b7435a8a919732f804854. This regenerated audit treats 28405b82d54b961459842d77f1547e3a37fa5bb1ce2b7435a8a919732f804854 as the exact audited pack hash and records 44a9b5fb3f3748c0e8a7bc80a4134fe3cb062dd66807ca20a671cb980529b7c6 as the historical review-reference hash.

visibilityTierLegend.USER_TACTICAL: Named concepts appropriate for direct user-facing recipes.
visibilityTierLegend.AGENT_COMPOSABLE: Safe building blocks Hermes may use in experimental plans after their contracts are complete.
visibilityTierLegend.COMPILER_LOWERING: Low-level geometry, kinematics, predicate, or source-fact lowering vocabulary not normally shown in recipe discovery.
visibilityTierLegend.TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY: High-level bundled logic usable only inside a reviewed recipe until hidden dependencies are formalized or split.
visibilityTierLegend.LEGACY_DOCUMENTATION: Historical or compatibility-only behavior, not a current authoring target.

standardLibraryExtensionChecklist: at least one real tactical consumer

hiddenDependencyTreatments.note: Formalize all shared runtime parameters in the catalog contract before broad direct Hermes exposure; existing reviewed recipes may keep supplying them at recipe level.
Keep restricted until its actual detector-stage inputs and selection policy are explicit, or split it into lower-level reusable capabilities.
Keep restricted because this is high-level M1 recipe logic, not a general primitive contract.
Make shared timing and internal geometric lowering dependencies explicit without exposing raw coordinate internals as user-facing recipe vocabulary.
Document this as an input-contract adapter for the same relation, not a separate football concept.

deferredNearTermCandidates.reason: Valuable and likely necessary soon, but may be folded into support_arrival_relation or added immediately after the second-family package proves the exact region/count contracts.
Wait for wide-isolation, pressing, and counterpress query work so the pressure metric is not overfit or overclaimed.

implementationBoundary: Do not implement these during the audit. The recommendations are planning candidates, not authorization for a broad primitive campaign.
